title: The Morning Fox

lead.headline: U.S.-Iran Deal Signing Set for Friday Amid Shipping Confusion and Israeli Strikes
lead.deck: Mixed signals over the U.S. naval blockade and continued Israeli strikes in Lebanon are disrupting shipping and threatening the planned accord.
lead.body: President Trump announced a U.S.-Iran agreement on Sunday and posted on Truth Social that he would "authorize the immediate removal of the United States Naval blockade," but later said the Strait of Hormuz would only reopen after the memorandum of understanding is signed on Friday. A multilateral naval command confirmed Monday the blockade remained in place. By Tuesday, a senior Iranian diplomat said the blockade had been lifted, and CBS News tracked at least four sanctioned Iranian-owned vessels crossing the blockade line since Sunday. Major shipping companies, including BIMCO members, have declined to resume normal operations pending clear terms and safety guarantees. Israeli warplanes struck the Nabatieh al-Fawqa area and the Ansariyeh town in south Lebanon on Wednesday, Lebanon's National News Agency reported. Trump criticized Prime Minister Netanyahu's strikes, calling them "vicious" and "too much." Iran has stated that continued Israeli troops and attacks in Lebanon would violate the agreement.
at_a_glance: Deal signing scheduled for Friday; blockade status disputed.
At least 4 Iranian vessels crossed blockade line since Sunday.
Israeli airstrikes hit south Lebanon Wednesday.

panels.lede_headline: Trump blocks Clayton DNI nomination, ties FISA renewal to voter ID bill
panels.lede_body: President Trump announced Wednesday he is delaying Jay Clayton's Senate confirmation as director of national intelligence, keeping Bill Pulte in the acting role. Trump said he will not approve renewal of the lapsed FISA surveillance program unless Congress passes the Save America Act, a voter ID bill that lacks sufficient support in either chamber. Clayton had been set to appear before the Intelligence Committee on Wednesday, with a vote expected Thursday.

also.headline: Fed holds rates at Warsh's first meeting
also.body: The Federal Reserve is expected to hold its key rate at 3.6% Wednesday · the fourth consecutive meeting without a change · Kevin Warsh chairs his first policy meeting and press conference.

also.headline: 15 Minnesotans charged with obstructing ICE agents
also.body: The Minnesota U.S. Attorney's Office charged 15 people Tuesday · allegations include coordinating blockades and training protesters in de-arrest tactics · protests outside St. Paul federal courthouse drew hundreds.

also.headline: Private jet crashes on Laredo highway, kills 1
also.body: A six-person private jet crashed on Loop 20 in Laredo, Texas, shortly before 10 p.m. Tuesday · one person killed · five first responders hospitalized with smoke inhalation.

also.headline: Wahab advances in California's 14th District special primary
also.body: CBS News projected state Sen. Aisha Wahab advanced Tuesday in the special primary for California's 14th Congressional District · the seat was vacated after Rep. Eric Swalwell resigned in April · a runoff is set for August 18 if no candidate tops 50%.

panels.lede_headline: VW Uses Sealed Bids to Manage Conflicts in $10 Billion Engines Sale
panels.lede_body: Volkswagen has turned to a sealed-bid process for the sale of its engines division, valued at approximately $10 billion, to prevent conflicts among bidders. The move reflects the complexity of divesting a core combustion-engine business as the automaker restructures amid declining internal-combustion demand and ongoing cost pressures across its European operations.

also.headline: Snap launches consumer AR glasses at $2,195
also.body: Snap released its Specs AR glasses on June 17, 2026, priced at $2,195 · Meta holds roughly 70% of the smart-glasses market with 3.5 million Ray-Ban units shipped.

also.headline: IEA warns Middle East peace deal risks oil glut
also.body: The IEA said · a Middle East peace deal could produce an oil supply glut in 2027 · the forecast implies downward pressure on crude prices.

also.headline: Investors increase bullish dollar bets on US strength
also.body: Investors have piled into long-dollar positions as the 'US exceptionalism' trade revived · the shift reflects renewed confidence in US economic outperformance relative to peers.

also.headline: JLR targets wealthy US buyers with hybrid, petrol cars
also.body: Jaguar Land Rover announced it will focus its US strategy on high-net-worth consumers · the company is prioritising hybrid and petrol models over electric vehicles.

panels.lede_headline: Z.ai releases GLM-5.2, MIT-licensed model with 1M-token context
panels.lede_body: Z.ai released GLM-5.2 on June 15–16, 2026 under an MIT open-source license. The 744-billion-parameter model targets coding and long-horizon agentic tasks with a 1M-token context window and two reasoning modes. On FrontierSWE it trails Claude Opus 4.8 by 1% while beating GPT-5.5 by 1%. Pricing is $1.40 per 1M input tokens and $4.40 per 1M output tokens.

also.headline: Pinterest launches conversational AI shopping app
also.body: Pinterest announced Ask Pinterest on June 17, 2026 · the standalone app uses its Taste Graph for personalized, natural-language product discovery · initial access is limited.

also.headline: CyberEvolver paper improves LLM security-agent success rates
also.body: Arxiv preprint revised June 16, 2026 · CyberEvolver raised seed-agent success rates by 13.6% on average · tested on CTF, exploitation, and pen-testing tasks across four open-source LLMs.

also.headline: AWS Strands SDK integrates LeRobot for hardware deployment
also.body: AWS open-sourced Strands Robots under Apache 2.0 · the SDK wraps LeRobot as AgentTools · one agent loop covers simulation recording, Hub push, and physical SO-101 robot deployment.

also.headline: GLM-5.2 scores 51 on Intelligence Index, costs $867 to evaluate
also.body: Artificial Analysis scores GLM-5.2 (max) at 51 on its Intelligence Index · average for comparable models is 24 · full evaluation cost $867.88 · throughput is 112 tokens per second.

panels.lede_headline: UK court sentences four Palestine Action members as terrorists
panels.lede_body: Woolwich Crown Court on June 12 convicted four Palestine Action members under counterterrorism legislation for a 2024 raid on an Elbit Systems factory in Filton that caused £1.2 million in damages. Charlotte Head and Leona Kamio received six-year sentences; Fatema Zainab Rajwani received five years and eight months; Samuel Corner received eight years and eight months. Police made more than 100 arrests outside the court during sentencing.

also.headline: Cambodia extradites scam syndicate leader to China
also.body: Beijing confirmed Wednesday · Cambodia extradited Liu Ren, a key member of the Chen Zhi crime syndicate · Liu's Jin Bei Group operated telecom fraud parks in Cambodia from 2016.

also.headline: Novo Nordisk hit by cyberattack, $25M ransom demanded
also.body: Hackergroup FulcrumSec claims to have stolen over one terabyte of Novo Nordisk data · the group demanded $25 million · Novo Nordisk confirmed the breach and said it is cooperating with authorities.

also.headline: Sri Lanka arrests ex-president's son on corruption charges
also.body: Yoshitha Rajapaksa, 38, was arrested June 17 · he is accused of using state funds for overseas naval training at Dartmouth without minimum qualifications · he was already subject to a foreign travel ban.

also.headline: Mumbai imposes water cuts amid 12-year rainfall low
also.body: Mumbai cut water to construction sites and reduced industrial supply by 20% on June 17 · reservoirs stand at 10.35% capacity, leaving 40 days of supply · Maharashtra received 75% below-average rainfall in June's first 16 days.

pull_quote: "Due to lack of details and a history of overly optimistic reassurances, we believe the security situation for the shipping industry remains volatile, and we still consider it very risky for ships to commence transits at this point." — Jakob Larsen, BIMCO

briefs.body: Approximately 1,500 ships remain stranded in the Persian Gulf despite a U.S.-Iran ceasefire agreement. Before the war, 140 vessels transited the Strait of Hormuz daily. U.S. forces are actively demining shipping lanes.

U.S. inflation reached 4.2% year-over-year in May 2026, the highest since 2023, driven by energy price spikes from the U.S.-Iran war. New Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh holds his first policy meeting today.

CISA added CVE-2026-48907, a CVSS 10.0 flaw in Joomla's JCE editor, to its exploited vulnerabilities catalog. Federal agencies must patch by June 19. The flaw allows unauthenticated PHP code execution.

15 malicious plugins on the JetBrains Marketplace have exfiltrated AI API keys since October 2025. Two plugins exceed 25,000 downloads each. A separate attack compromised 144 Mastra npm packages on June 17.

NPR found federal prisons routinely reject medical grievances, delaying care for years. Congress granted ICE over $113 billion since last year; detention complaints citing spoiled food and denied healthcare are rising.

The Bellingshausen Sea is missing approximately 650,000 square kilometers of winter sea ice — roughly the area of France — compared to the 1991–2020 average, marking the third such deficit in four years.

data_point.context: U.S. inflation rate as of the latest reading, a three-year high, driven largely by higher gas prices stemming from the Iran war, per Federal Reserve policymakers cited by WCVB.

on_this_day.body: On June 17, 1972, five men were arrested inside the Democratic National Committee headquarters at the Watergate complex in Washington, D.C. The break-in, later linked to the Nixon re-election campaign, led to a congressional investigation and Nixon's resignation on August 9, 1974.
